title: Inspect & Analyze the Data
intro:
STEP 4 OF 8 · MODULE 4

Inspect & Analyze the Data

Mission: Produce a complete, honest, documented picture of the vehicle’s true health.

OBJECTIVE
VEHICLE HEALTH
TOOL
DVI & PHOTOS
PRIMARY KPI
DVI W/ MEDIA %
FAILURE MODE
PARTIAL DVI

sections.title: WHY Inspection Exists — It’s Not About the Repair
sections.html:
WHY Inspection Exists — It’s Not About the Repair

The inspection is not for the technician. The technician already knows what is wrong with the car. The inspection — specifically the digital inspection with photos, video, and notes — is for the customer. It is a piece of evidence that we will hand them in Step 6 (Present) so they can see what we see. Without that evidence, every repair we recommend lands as opinion. With it, every recommendation lands as fact.

The DVI is not a checklist. It is the X-ray we hand the customer so they stop being afraid of the diagnosis.

sections.title: The Vehicle Health Dashboard — Green / Yellow / Red
sections.html:
The Vehicle Health Dashboard — Green / Yellow / Red

The DVI is just the inspection sections of the vehicle. Within those sections, every finding gets classified against one question: "Does it operate within manufacturer specifications?" The answer drives the color, and the color drives the conversation.

Figure 4.1 · Vehicle Health Dashboard — one question, three answers, three different conversations
PRO TIPYellow is not a sale — it's a flag.

Yellow items are not presented as a sales opportunity today. They are documented for re-inspection at the next visit, or rolled into a scheduled-maintenance conversation on 3rd base if they're approaching a service interval. Treating Yellow as a sale erodes the "we'll tell you what we see" trust we earned in Module 2. Treat Yellow as a watch — the customer will trust you more for it.

PRO TIPRed is a finding, not a base assignment.

Red means exactly one thing: the item is not operating within the manufacturer’s specification. It does not automatically mean “sell it on 1st base.” 1st base belongs to the customer’s original concern — or an immediate safety or reliability threat if they came in with no concern. Every other Red finding gets carried into Build & Strategize (Module 5) and plugged into the base plan: a discovery beyond the original concern is a 2nd-base expansion, and a Red item that fits the maintenance picture can ride on 3rd base. Sort the finding by spec. Sell it by strategy.

sections.title: How Inspection Creates Trust — The Reciprocity of Effort
sections.html:
How Inspection Creates Trust — The Reciprocity of Effort

When a customer sees that we have spent an hour inspecting, photographing, video-recording, and documenting their vehicle — before we ever ask them for a dollar — the brain registers a debt. The customer’s subconscious thought: “these people did all that for me before I authorized anything — the least I can do is hear them out.” This is reciprocity at industrial scale.

PSYCHOLOGY DECODERWhy a thorough DVI shortens objections.

An objection like “the price seems high” is almost always shorthand for “I’m not sure this is real.” A DVI with photos, video, and tech notes destroys the “not real” doubt before the objection forms. Shops with DVI-completion rates above 95% report dramatically lower price-objection rates — not because their prices are lower, but because their evidence is higher.

sections.title: Tech-Advisor Communication — The Hand-Off That Makes or Breaks the Sale
sections.html:
Tech-Advisor Communication — The Hand-Off That Makes or Breaks the Sale

The tech sees the car. The advisor sells the car. If they don’t sync, the customer hears guesses. The hand-off protocol:

  1. Walk the bay. Advisor physically meets the tech at the vehicle within 15 minutes of DVI completion.
  2. Listen first. Tech explains the finding. Advisor takes notes. No interruptions.
  3. Translate. Advisor reflects the tech’s language back in customer-friendly terms. Tech corrects if anything is off.
  4. Confirm PRS. Together: Problem, Risk, Solution, Investment, Warranty.
  5. Sign off. Both initial the RO so accountability is shared.

sections.title: Mastery Check
sections.html:
Mastery Check

LEVEL 4 · INSPECTION MASTERY

  • DVI completed bumper-to-bumper, every section.
  • Every Yellow and Red finding has at least one photo or video.
  • Tech walk-the-bay completed within 15 minutes of DVI completion.
  • Customer sent DVI via text/email and viewed (confirmed) before presentation call.
  • PRS drafted for every Red and Yellow finding before any callback.
COACH’S CORNERAudit two DVIs per advisor per week.

Open the DVI as if you were the customer. Could you understand the finding from the photos and notes alone? Is any finding missing media? Most failures here are caught and corrected inside one cycle.

DVI Completion %
100%
% of vehicles in the bay that get a full DVI before any callback.
DVI w/ Media %
≥ 95%
% of Yellow/Red findings with photo or video evidence.
DVI View-Rate
